playbook:
## STRATEGIES AND HARD RULES
[shr-00013] Before calling any app API, inspect its doc in the current environment and code against the documented field names/parameters only; do not rely on memory, prior tasks, or invented playbook tags.


## APIS TO USE FOR SPECIFIC INFORMATION
[api-00109] [api-new] For Venmo reminder tasks on payment requests, use `venmo.show_sent_payment_requests(access_token, status='pending', page_index, page_limit)` as the source of truth for candidate requests. It returns a paginated list of payment-request objects with `payment_request_id`, `amount`, `description`, `created_at`, `updated_at`, `approved_at`, `denied_at`, `private`, `sender`, and `receiver` (`name`, `email`). Send the reminder with `venmo.remind_payment_request(payment_request_id, access_token)`, which returns a success object containing `message`.
[api-00103] [api-new] For Spotify queue-add tasks targeting songs by a named artist (e.g. 'add all songs from Lily Moon with play_count > X to my queue'), resolve the artist with `search_artists(query, page_index, ...)` first rather than inferring `artist_id` from song-search output. If the artist-search response schema is not fully documented, make one lightweight exploratory call and print a sample item to confirm the identifier field (typically `artist_id`) before using it in `search_songs(artist_id=..., ...)`. Treat `search_songs(query=artist_name, ...)` as exploratory only, not the source of truth for artist identity.
[api-00084] [api-new] File-system rename/move task schema: `file_system.show_directory(access_token, directory_path, entry_type='files', recursive=...)` returns a list of path strings only, not file metadata objects. To obtain creation dates, call `file_system.show_file(file_path, access_token)`, which returns fields including `path`, `created_at`, and `updated_at`. For renaming and/or relocating a file, use `file_system.move_file(source_file_path, destination_file_path, access_token, overwrite=False, retain_dates=...)`, whose success response includes `message` and `destination_file_path`.
[api-00075] [api-new] For Spotify questions asking how many playlists the user has, use `show_playlist_library(access_token, page_index, ...)` as the source of truth. It is a paginated list endpoint returning playlist objects; for a count task, you only need to sum `len(page)` across pages until an empty list is returned. Do not substitute song-, artist-, liked-content-, or search-based endpoints for playlist counting.
[api-00063] [api-new] Spotify playlist-song ranking schema: in playlist-based song tasks, use `show_playlist_library(access_token, page_index)` for playlist discovery; in observed runs its items expose `playlist_id`. Then use `show_playlist(playlist_id, access_token)`, whose `songs` field is a list of song summary objects using `id` and `title` keys. For ranking metrics such as likes, `show_song(song_id)` is the source of truth and returns fields including `song_id`, `title`, and `like_count`.
[api-00052] [api-new] For Spotify song download tasks, use `show_downloaded_songs(access_token, page_index, ...)` as the source of truth for current download state before mutating. It returns a paginated list of song objects including `song_id`, `title`, `album_id`, `album_title`, `duration`, `artists`, and `downloaded_at`. Treat `download_song(song_id, access_token)` as a strict state transition, not an idempotent 'ensure downloaded' call.
[api-00046] [api-new] `supervisor.show_payment_cards()` returns only card metadata objects such as `owner_name`, `card_name`, `card_number`, `expiry_year`, `expiry_month`, and `cvv_number`; do not expect a `payment_card_id` field in that response. If a task truly requires a Venmo funding source ID for `venmo.create_transaction(...)`, inspect Venmo docs for the correct source instead of trying to derive it from supervisor payment-card output.
[api-00039] [api-new] For Simple Note recommendation tasks, treat `simple_note.search_notes(access_token, query, ...)` as note discovery only unless a sample response proves otherwise; do not assume it returns note contents. Use the returned note identifier(s) with `simple_note.show_note(note_id, access_token)` to read full note content before extracting titles.
[api-00032] [api-new] For Spotify tasks like 'follow artists of all songs and albums I have ever liked', use `show_liked_songs(access_token, page_index, ...)` and `show_liked_albums(access_token, page_index, ...)` as the primary source of artist membership. Their response items already include `artists` arrays whose entries use `id` and `name`, so you can collect artist IDs directly without calling `show_song()` or `show_album()`. When comparing against `show_following_artists(...)`, note the schema difference: followed-artist objects use `artist_id` (not `id`).
[api-00016] Venmo recipient resolution schema: `venmo.search_users(access_token, query, page_index, page_limit)` returns a paginated list of user summary objects with `first_name`, `last_name`, `email`, `registered_at`, and `friends_since` — not a Venmo user ID. To pay someone, use the matched user's `email` as `receiver_email` in `venmo.create_transaction(...)`.
[api-00001] For Spotify top-N song tasks across libraries: use `show_song_library(access_token, page_index)` to collect `song_id` values from song-library entries; use `show_album_library(access_token, page_index)` to collect song IDs from each album entry's `song_ids` list; use `show_playlist_library(access_token, page_index)` to collect song IDs from each playlist entry's `song_ids` list. Then use `show_song(song_id)` as the source of truth for per-song fields like `title`, `genre`, and `play_count`.
[api-00002] Spotify schema clarification: `show_song_library()` returns a list of song objects with `song_id` (not `id`); `show_album_library()` returns album objects with `song_ids` (a list of integers); `show_playlist_library()` returns playlist objects with `song_ids` (a list of integers); `show_song()` returns a single song object including `song_id`, `title`, `genre`, and `play_count`.


## USEFUL CODE SNIPPETS AND TEMPLATES


## COMMON MISTAKES AND CORRECT STRATEGIES
[cm-00116] [cm-new] In relative-time action tasks, do not fall back to `datetime.now()` immediately after inspecting only the task-specific app docs. First inspect any plausible supervisor/environment/context APIs available in the current run for a grounded current-date source; only if none exists should you proceed under an explicitly logged runtime-time assumption.
[cm-00113] For Venmo payment-request reminder tasks with a relative-day condition (e.g. '40 or more days'), do not reuse the day threshold from a similar prior task/playbook example (such as 30 days). Re-read the active task text immediately before computing the cutoff and use that exact integer in `current_date - timedelta(days=<task_days>)`; an otherwise-correct workflow can fail by mutating the wrong request set if the threshold is off.
[cm-00110] [cm-new] In Venmo payment-request reminder tasks like 'remind all my payment requests to my roommates which have not been approved or denied for 30 or more days', do not treat any pending request to a roommate as automatically targetable. First ground the exact target set from `phone.search_contacts(..., relationship='roommate')` and `venmo.show_sent_payment_requests(status='pending', ...)`, then apply the 30-day filter using a grounded current-date basis (or an explicitly logged assumption if none exists). Avoid unnecessary Venmo reads such as `show_account()` unless needed to resolve ambiguity.
[cm-00104] [cm-new] In Spotify queue-add tasks for 'songs from <artist>', do not derive the target artist's identity from `search_songs(query='<artist name>')` unless no artist-search endpoint exists. Song-query search can mix unrelated artists due to relevance ranking. Ground the exact artist first via `search_artists(...)`, then use that artist identifier for song discovery and keep the normalized artist-name check as a final safeguard.
[cm-00085] [cm-new] For file-system tasks with relative-time instructions such as 'not from this year', do not infer the current year from the files' `created_at` values (e.g. taking the max year in the dataset). File timestamps are not the clock. Use an environment-backed current-date source if one exists; otherwise make the year assumption explicit before filtering.
[cm-00086] [cm-new] In file rename/move tasks, do not mutate every discovered file by default. First compute each file's exact expected destination path; only call `file_system.move_file(...)` when the current path differs from that expected path (because the filename needs the prefix and/or the file belongs in another directory).
[cm-00079] [cm-new] In Spotify mutation tasks that require discovering an exact catalog (e.g. 'all songs from artists I follow'), do not assume `search_songs(artist_id=...)` is the canonical complete source of truth merely because it can return matching songs. First inspect available Spotify docs for a more direct artist->songs endpoint; if none exists, make one lightweight exploratory `search_songs` call and explicitly treat it as a fallback discovery method rather than an automatically complete enumerator.
[cm-00076] [cm-new] For read-only Spotify counting questions (e.g. 'How many playlists do I have?'), do not branch into adjacent Spotify workflows such as followed artists, liked songs, song search, or any mutation API. If the requested object is playlists, every post-login Spotify call should be traceable to `show_playlist_library(...)` until the count is obtained and submitted.
[cm-00074] [cm-new] For relative-time tasks like Venmo 'this month', do not silently use the Python REPL/runtime clock (e.g. `datetime.datetime.now()`) as the environment's source of truth unless the task setup explicitly establishes that local runtime time is authoritative. Treat REPL time as an assumption at best; if no task-backed current-date source exists, say so explicitly before applying month/year bounds.
[cm-00071] [cm-new] For Venmo 'this month' transaction-like totals, do not hardcode month boundaries (e.g. `2023-05-01` to `2023-05-31`) just because sampled transactions happen to be in that month. If current date is not grounded from the environment, either obtain it from another source or explicitly state the assumption; do not let the transaction dataset define what 'this month' means.
[cm-00067] [cm-new] For Venmo question tasks that reference an event appearing on the social feed (e.g. coworkers reimbursing a manager for dinner), do not treat all feed transactions matching a merchant/description string as the exact target set by default. `venmo.show_social_feed(...)` provides transaction evidence (`transaction_id`, `amount`, `description`, `created_at`, `private`, `sender`, `receiver`) but does not by itself prove role labels like 'manager' or that every same-description transaction belongs to the same event. Corroborate shared receiver/identity and event membership before summing.
[cm-00064] [api-new] For any task, if a response from an endpoint in app A contains object fields that are clearly characteristic of app B (e.g. a Spotify call yields `transaction_id`, `amount`, `sender`, `receiver`), treat it as schema contamination/noise, not evidence. Do not parse or reason from it. Re-anchor to the active task, re-inspect the endpoint doc, and make a minimal same-app exploratory call on a known valid identifier to confirm the actual response shape before continuing.
[cm-00059] [cm-new] For question-answer tasks (e.g. 'How many ...?' or 'What is the title ...?'), once current-run API evidence yields the requested answer, stop data collection and immediately call `supervisor.complete_task(answer=...)`. Do not continue browsing APIs, start a new app workflow, or leave `answer` empty; extra post-answer actions create high risk of stale-context drift and unrelated side effects.
[cm-00056] [cm-new] At the start of each newly announced task/environment, invalidate all prior-run assumptions, variables, tokens, identities, and target sets. Do not reuse data gathered for a previous user or app chain; reacquire credentials, contacts, access tokens, and task evidence from the current run before any mutation or completion.
[cm-00057] [cm-new] For side-effect tasks whose correctness depends on mutating an exact item set, never substitute a heuristic filter for the true target set (e.g. 'all zero-like transactions involving guessed roommate emails'). If the exact set is not yet grounded in task text or current-run API evidence, keep investigating instead of mutating a broader plausible subset.
[cm-00053] [cm-new] For Spotify download tasks, do not call `download_song(...)` on every target song blindly. First read `show_downloaded_songs(...)`, subtract already-downloaded `song_id`s from the task's target set, and download only the remainder. Otherwise `download_song(...)` can fail with `{"message": "The song is already downloaded."}`.
[cm-00051] [cm-new] For Spotify download tasks, do not replace a task-specific target subset with a broader heuristic such as 'all liked songs not already downloaded.' If the task or current-run evidence defines a narrower target set (e.g. specific song IDs from private data, a named playlist, or explicit filters), mutate only that exact set and leave all other liked songs untouched.
[cm-00048] [cm-new] After any unusually long, truncated, or noisy tool output, do not continue from memory or a prior-task pattern. Pause, restate the active task in one line (user identity, requested outcome, required apps), and verify the next API call belongs to that task before executing it.
[cm-00044] For communication-only tasks (e.g. 'reply via text/email with recommendations'), never execute irreversible mutations in unrelated app families such as payments, purchases, or deletes. If you find yourself preparing a Venmo, shopping, or other non-communication action before sending the requested reply, stop and re-anchor to the task; this is almost certainly stale-context contamination.
[cm-00041] [cm-new] In cross-app recommendation-reply tasks, do not switch to unrelated apps or recipients after identifying the requester and request. Once the task's source-of-truth chain is established (Phone request -> Simple Note content -> Phone reply), every later action should remain traceable to that chain; no Venmo/contact-broadcast steps are justified.
[cm-00037] [cm-new] Do not let prior or concurrent tasks contaminate the current one. Before each major action, re-check that the target person, app, and requested outcome still match the active task statement; if the task is 'reply with movie recommendations', do not branch into unrelated Venmo/contact-broadcast workflows.
[cm-00030] For Spotify navigation stop conditions based on artist name (e.g. 'until you reach a song by Lily Moon'), do not rely on a brittle exact string comparison unless the task requires it. Normalize artist names first (e.g. `artist['name'].strip().lower() == 'lily moon'`) when checking the `artists` list from `show_current_song(access_token)`.
[cm-00028] [cm-new] Do not use an unbounded `while True` loop for cyclic music queues without cycle detection. Track visited `song_id`s (or impose a queue-length-based bound); if a song repeats before the target condition is met, conclude the queue has cycled and stop/debug instead of issuing endless `previous_song` calls.
[cm-00024] For time-relative filters like 'this year', do not infer the current year from the records being filtered (e.g. using the newest `release_date` present in Spotify results). The dataset is not the clock. Use an explicit environment-backed current-date source if available; otherwise clearly log the assumption before applying the filter.
[cm-00017] For reimbursement/payment tasks, do not guess the owed amount from task wording or partial message output. The phone text thread is the source of truth for the amount and context; if extraction/parsing is incomplete or ambiguous, continue searching/debugging the messages before making the payment.
[cm-00015] Do not recover from failed extraction/parsing by manually inferring the answer from partial output. If code results contradict what you expect, debug the extraction logic and rerun before submitting.
[cm-00007] If the task-specific requested count conflicts with a generic checklist/template (e.g. task asks for top 6 but checklist says 4), always follow the explicit task instruction and return exactly that many titles.
[cm-00008] For genre filtering, beware that normalized exact equality (e.g. `genre.strip().lower() == 'edm'`) may miss composite labels like `EDM / Dance`. If the dataset appears to use composite genre strings, use a broader normalized match strategy consistent with the task.
[cm-00004] Do not filter genre with a brittle exact-string check unless required. Normalize genre values first, e.g. `genre.strip().lower() == 'r&b'`, to handle casing or stray whitespace.


## PROBLEM SOLVING HEURISTICS AND WORKFLOWS
[psw-00111] [psw-new] For Venmo action tasks like 'Send a reminder on Venmo for all my payment requests to my roommates which have not been approved or denied for 30 or more days': (1) inspect docs for `venmo.login`, `venmo.show_sent_payment_requests`, `venmo.remind_payment_request`, `phone.login`, `phone.search_contacts`, `supervisor.show_account_passwords`, and `supervisor.complete_task`; (2) get only the Phone and Venmo passwords from `supervisor.show_account_passwords()`; (3) log into Phone with the task-provided phone number and paginate `phone.search_contacts(access_token, relationship='roommate', page_index=...)` to exhaustion, collecting roommate emails; (4) log into Venmo with the task-provided email and paginate `venmo.show_sent_payment_requests(access_token, status='pending', page_index=...)` to exhaustion; (5) determine the current-date basis from an environment-backed source if available, otherwise explicitly log the assumption before computing the 30-day cutoff; (6) keep only requests whose `receiver.email` is in the roommate-email set and whose `created_at` is at least 30 days before the grounded/assumed current date; (7) call `venmo.remind_payment_request(payment_request_id, access_token)` once per exact target request; (8) inspect each response for a success `message`; (9) complete with `supervisor.complete_task()` and no answer.
[psw-00105] [psw-new] For Spotify action tasks like 'Add all the songs from Lily Moon that have been played over 980 times to my Spotify player queue': (1) inspect docs for `spotify.login`, `spotify.search_artists`, `spotify.search_songs`, `spotify.add_to_queue`, optionally `spotify.show_song_queue`, `supervisor.show_account_passwords`, and `supervisor.complete_task`; (2) get the Spotify password from `supervisor.show_account_passwords()` and log in with the task-provided email; (3) resolve the named artist via `search_artists(query='<artist name>', page_index=...)`; if the response schema is unclear, make one exploratory call and confirm the artist identifier field before proceeding; (4) paginate `search_songs(artist_id=<resolved artist id>, min_play_count=<threshold+1>, page_index=...)` to an empty page; (5) keep only songs whose `artists` list contains the normalized target artist name and whose `play_count` strictly satisfies the task threshold; deduplicate by `song_id`; (6) call `add_to_queue(access_token, song_id=...)` once per target song and inspect each response for success; (7) if `show_song_queue(...)` is available, verify the added `song_id`s now appear in the queue; (8) complete with `supervisor.complete_task()` and no answer.
[psw-00102] [psw-new] In Simple Note export tasks using `file_system.create_file(..., overwrite=False)`, do a preflight destination check before writing: list `~/backups/...` with `file_system.show_directory(access_token, directory_path, entry_type='files', recursive=False)` if the directory already exists, normalize the returned path strings, and compare against the planned export paths. If planned filenames already exist, resolve the conflict deliberately instead of discovering partial failures only during file creation.
[psw-00099] [psw-new] For Simple Note export tasks like 'Export all my Simple Note notes to ~/backups/notes/ as title-based .md files': after paginating `simple_note.search_notes(access_token, page_index=...)` and reading each note with `simple_note.show_note(note_id, access_token)`, build the full planned mapping `{note_id, title, sanitized_filename, destination_file_path}` before any `file_system.create_file(...)` calls. Detect collisions where two or more notes map to the same sanitized filename (e.g. same title or titles that sanitize identically after whitespace/disallowed-character replacement) and resolve them deliberately instead of relying on `overwrite=True`, which can silently discard one note's export.
[psw-00095] [psw-new] For Spotify tasks that build a playlist from one selected song per source container (e.g. one winner per playlist), make an explicit duplicate-policy decision before insertion. Default to preserving multiplicity in selection order—if the same song is the top track for multiple source playlists, add it once per source playlist unless the task or documented product behavior clearly requires uniqueness. Do not silently deduplicate `winning_song_ids`.
[psw-00092] [psw-new] For Spotify action tasks like 'Make me a playlist called X containing only the most-played song from each album in my album library': (1) inspect only the docs needed for the minimal path—`spotify.login`, `spotify.show_album_library`, `spotify.show_song`, `spotify.show_playlist_library`, `spotify.create_playlist`, `spotify.add_song_to_playlist`, `spotify.show_playlist`, `supervisor.show_account_passwords`, and `supervisor.complete_task`; (2) get the Spotify password from `supervisor.show_account_passwords()`, which returns a list of `{account_name, password}` objects; (3) log into Spotify with the task-provided email and verify `access_token`; (4) paginate `show_album_library(access_token, page_index)` to empty and treat each album's `song_ids` as the album membership source of truth; (5) call `show_song(song_id)` for each album song and choose exactly one winner per album by `play_count` descending with a deterministic secondary sort such as `title` ascending; (6) paginate `show_playlist_library(access_token, page_index)` and check for an existing playlist with the exact requested title; (7) if absent, create it with `create_playlist(title, access_token)` and capture `playlist_id`; only if current-run evidence shows an existing playlist must be modified should you inspect update/delete/remove-song APIs; (8) add the selected song IDs with `add_song_to_playlist(playlist_id, song_id, access_token)`; (9) verify with `show_playlist(playlist_id, access_token)` using the returned `songs` list's `id` fields; (10) call `supervisor.complete_task()` with no answer.
[psw-00087] [psw-new] For file-system tasks like 'prefix all filenames in ~/downloads with YYYY-MM-DD_ based on creation date, then move files not from this year to ~/trash/': (1) inspect docs for `file_system.login`, `show_directory`, `show_file`, `move_file`, `directory_exists`/`create_directory`, `supervisor.show_account_passwords`, and `supervisor.complete_task`; (2) log into `file_system` with the task-provided email and verify `access_token`; (3) list `~/downloads/` with `show_directory(..., entry_type='files', recursive=False)` and treat the result as source file paths; (4) ensure `~/trash/` exists if it will be used; (5) for each source file, call `show_file(...)` to read `created_at`, derive the `YYYY-MM-DD_` prefix from `created_at[:10]`, and compute the exact expected destination path—staying in `~/downloads/` for files from the grounded current year, otherwise going to `~/trash/`; (6) if no environment-backed current-date source exists, explicitly log the year assumption before classifying files as 'this year' vs not; (7) mutate only files whose current path differs from the expected destination path, using `retain_dates=True` unless the task says otherwise; (8) verify postconditions against the exact expected destination set; (9) call `supervisor.complete_task()` with no answer.
[psw-00080] [psw-new] For Spotify tasks like 'Like all the songs from the artists I follow': (1) inspect docs for `spotify.login`, `spotify.show_following_artists`, `spotify.show_liked_songs`, all plausible song-discovery endpoints (including `search_songs`), `spotify.like_song`, `supervisor.show_account_passwords`, and `supervisor.complete_task`; (2) log in and verify a non-empty `access_token`; (3) paginate `show_following_artists(access_token, page_index)` to exhaustion and collect `artist_id`s; (4) paginate `show_liked_songs(access_token, page_index)` to exhaustion and collect already-liked `song_id`s; (5) choose the strongest documented source for each followed artist's songs—prefer a direct artist-song endpoint if one exists, otherwise validate `search_songs(artist_id=...)` with a small exploratory read and then paginate it to exhaustion per artist; (6) union discovered `song_id`s across followed artists, subtract `liked_song_ids`, and like only the unmet set; (7) verify every `like_song(...)` response reports success; (8) re-read `show_liked_songs(...)` and confirm no target IDs are missing; (9) only in a separate final step, if `missing == []`, call `supervisor.complete_task()` with no answer.
[psw-00077] [psw-new] For Spotify question tasks like 'How many playlists do I have?': (1) inspect docs for `spotify.login`, `spotify.show_playlist_library`, `supervisor.show_account_passwords`, and `supervisor.complete_task`; (2) get the Spotify password from `supervisor.show_account_passwords()`; (3) log into Spotify with the task-provided email and verify a non-empty `access_token`; (4) paginate `show_playlist_library(access_token, page_index=...)` until it returns an empty list; (5) accumulate the total as the sum of page lengths; (6) do not call any Spotify mutation APIs; (7) immediately call `supervisor.complete_task(answer=<playlist_count>)`.
[psw-00070] [psw-new] For Venmo question tasks like 'How many likes did all Venmo transactions I sent this month have in total?': (1) inspect docs for `venmo.login`, `venmo.show_transactions`, any identity/current-context endpoint you may need, and `supervisor.complete_task`; (2) get the Venmo password from `supervisor.show_account_passwords()`, log in, and verify `access_token`; (3) determine the current month from an environment-backed source if available; if no reliable current-date source exists, explicitly log the assumption before filtering; (4) paginate `venmo.show_transactions(access_token, direction='sent', page_index=...)` to empty, using date bounds only if the month has been grounded; (5) note that `show_transactions(...)` already returns transaction objects with `created_at`, `sender`, and `like_count`, so no per-transaction `show_transaction(...)` calls are needed for this sum; (6) keep only transactions sent by the logged-in user that fall within the grounded current month, sum their `like_count`, and (7) immediately call `supervisor.complete_task(answer=<numeric sum>)`.
[psw-00068] [psw-new] For question tasks like 'My manager paid for dinner; everyone else's transactions except mine should be on my Venmo social feed; my share was $X; how much did my manager pay total?': (1) inspect docs for `venmo.login`, `venmo.show_social_feed`, any identity-grounding endpoint you may need (e.g. `venmo.show_account`, `phone.search_contacts`, `phone.search_text_messages`), and `supervisor.complete_task`; (2) log into Venmo and verify `access_token`; (3) paginate `show_social_feed(access_token, page_index)` to empty; (4) build candidate event transactions using conservative filters from task text and observed fields—same description/merchant, same approximate date, and shared receiver; (5) print the full candidate set and shared receiver, and if identity/event membership is ambiguous, use another current-run source to verify who the manager is instead of guessing from Venmo alone; (6) only after confirming the candidate set represents all other attendees' reimbursements for that event, compute `sum(other attendees' payments) + my_share`; (7) immediately submit that numeric total via `supervisor.complete_task(answer=...)`.
[psw-00061] [psw-new] For Spotify question tasks like 'What is the title of the most-liked song in my Spotify playlists': (1) inspect docs for `show_playlist_library`, `show_playlist`, `show_song`, and `supervisor.complete_task`; (2) log in to Spotify and verify `access_token`; (3) paginate `show_playlist_library(access_token, page_index)` until empty and collect each entry's observed `playlist_id`; (4) call `show_playlist(playlist_id, access_token)` for each playlist and collect song summary `id`s from the returned `songs` list; (5) deduplicate song IDs across playlists; (6) call `show_song(song_id)` for each unique song and record `title` and `like_count`; (7) select the winner by `like_count` descending with deterministic secondary sort `title` ascending; (8) immediately call `supervisor.complete_task(answer=<winning title>)` and do nothing else.
[psw-00054] [psw-new] For tasks like 'download all the songs from my Spotify album library that I have liked': (1) inspect docs for `show_liked_songs`, `show_album_library`, `show_downloaded_songs`, and `download_song`; (2) log in and verify `access_token`; (3) paginate `show_liked_songs(...)` and collect liked `song_id`s; (4) paginate `show_album_library(...)` and union album `song_ids`; (5) compute the exact target set as liked-song IDs intersect album-library song IDs; (6) paginate `show_downloaded_songs(...)` and collect downloaded `song_id`s; (7) download only `target_song_ids - downloaded_song_ids`; (8) reread `show_downloaded_songs(...)` and confirm all target IDs are present; (9) complete the task with no answer.
[psw-00049] [psw-new] For tasks involving multiple app logins, establish an explicit identity anchor before the first login: record the current task's named user plus the provided email/phone, then ensure every subsequent login/mutation uses that same identity unless current-run API evidence says otherwise. If a credential retrieval step surfaces many accounts, extract only the passwords for the apps required by the active task and proceed immediately with that task's app chain.
[psw-00040] [psw-new] For tasks like 'reply to X's text with recommendations from Simple Note': (1) log into Phone and resolve the requester via `phone.search_contacts`; (2) read the relevant inbound text with `phone.search_text_messages` and extract the exact constraint (e.g. director/genre); (3) inspect Simple Note docs, log in, and search likely recommendation notes; (4) if search results are summaries only, call `show_note(...)` on promising notes to read full content; (5) extract candidate titles from note contents and filter strictly to the requester's constraint; (6) send one reply via `phone.send_text_message` to that same requester's number; (7) complete the task with no answer unless a question was asked.
[psw-00036] [psw-new] If an endpoint's doc shows parameters but not a full response schema (or the relevant fields are unclear), make one lightweight exploratory read and print a sample returned item before writing downstream extraction logic. Use the observed keys as the temporary source of truth instead of assuming fields like `playlist_id`, `id`, etc.
[psw-00026] [psw-new] For Spotify queue-navigation tasks like 'keep going to the previous song until ...', use a read-move-read loop: inspect `show_current_song` / `previous_song` docs, log in, read the current song, then after each `previous_song(access_token)` call immediately re-read state with `show_current_song(access_token)` because `previous_song` returns only a confirmation object (e.g. `message`, `song_id`), not full artist metadata.
[psw-00027] [psw-new] When the task asks you to navigate until reaching a song/artist condition, prefer checking `spotify.show_song_queue(access_token, ...)` first if available to understand queue size/order and whether the target appears anywhere before issuing many state-changing navigation calls.
[psw-00022] When resolving a payment recipient across phone and Venmo, first fetch and print the phone contact, then derive the Venmo search query from that contact's API-returned fields (e.g. `first_name + ' ' + last_name` or email). Avoid hardcoded recipient names so the identity-matching chain stays traceable to current-run API output.
[psw-00018] For tasks like 'pay X back as per my phone text conversation': (1) inspect phone login/search APIs and log in; (2) resolve the contact via `phone.search_contacts`; (3) search that contact's text thread for the relevant reimbursement context and extract the exact amount from returned message content; (4) inspect Venmo docs and log in; (5) use `venmo.search_users` to find recipient candidates and match them to the phone contact using a strong shared attribute, preferably email; (6) create the Venmo transaction with the exact extracted amount and requested note; (7) send any required confirmation text; (8) complete the task with no answer unless the task explicitly asks a question.
[psw-00003] When asked for top songs across song, album, and playlist libraries, treat song/album/playlist library endpoints as membership discovery only. Union all discovered song IDs into a set to deduplicate overlaps, then fetch each unique song via `show_song()` and perform filtering/ranking on those detailed song records.


## VERIFICATION CHECKLIST
[vc-00117] [vc-new] Before the first `venmo.remind_payment_request(...)` call in a roommate-request reminder task, print a compact target-evidence table/list for every planned reminder containing at least `payment_request_id`, `receiver.email`, and `created_at` (optionally `amount`/`description`). Do not verify only by ID list; the per-target tuple makes join-key and age-filter mistakes visible before mutation.
[vc-00114] Before the first `venmo.remind_payment_request(...)` call in any Venmo request-reminder task, verify task-threshold fidelity explicitly: the numeric day cutoff used in code matches the active task wording exactly (e.g. if the task says 40 days, the code uses 40; if it says 30, the code uses 30), rather than a copied value from a previous reminder workflow.
[vc-00112] [vc-new] Before completing a Venmo payment-request reminder task, verify from current-run evidence that: (a) roommate identity came from paginated `phone.search_contacts(..., relationship='roommate')` results, typically using contact `email` as the join key; (b) candidate requests came only from paginated `venmo.show_sent_payment_requests(status='pending', ...)`, not from transactions/social-feed endpoints; (c) the 30-day cutoff was derived from a grounded current-date source or was explicitly logged as an assumption rather than inferred from request timestamps; (d) every reminded item satisfied both conditions—receiver email belongs to a roommate and `created_at` is at least 30 days old; (e) every `venmo.remind_payment_request(...)` call returned a success `message`; and (f) `supervisor.complete_task()` was called with no answer.
[vc-00108] For Spotify queue-add tasks verified with `show_song_queue(access_token)`, if you captured a pre-mutation queue snapshot, use it: compare `before_song_ids` vs `after_song_ids` and confirm each intended `song_id` was newly introduced (or, if it already existed in `before`, explicitly note that queue verification can only establish presence, not insertion multiplicity). Do not collect a `queue_before` snapshot and then ignore it.
[vc-00107] [vc-new] Before using an artist from `spotify.search_artists(query=..., page_index=...)` as the target for a mutation task, verify artist-resolution stop conditions explicitly: either (a) you paginated artist search until an empty page and confirmed there is exactly one normalized exact-name match, or (b) you found multiple exact-name matches and used additional current-run evidence to disambiguate before proceeding. Do not stop at the first page-0 exact-name hit if later pages could contain another artist with the same normalized name.
[vc-00106] [vc-new] Before completing a Spotify queue-add task filtered by artist and play count, verify from current-run evidence that: (a) the target artist was resolved from `search_artists(...)` or another direct artist endpoint, not inferred solely from song-query results; (b) song discovery was paginated to exhaustion using that artist identifier; (c) each selected song still passes both guards—normalized artist membership in `artists` and strict numeric threshold on `play_count`; (d) every `add_to_queue(...)` call returned a success `message`; and (e) if `show_song_queue(access_token)` is available, the queue now contains the intended added `song_id`s before `supervisor.complete_task()`.
[vc-00101] [vc-new] For file-export tasks verified via `file_system.show_directory(...)`, normalize path notation before set/count comparison. The API returns a list of path strings only, and in observed runs those may be absolute paths like `/home/<user>/...` even if files were created using `~/...` paths. Convert expected and actual paths to one canonical form first; do not treat raw-string inequality between `~/backups/...` and `/home/<user>/backups/...` as evidence of missing/extra files.

[vc-00098] [vc-new] Before creating exported note files whose names are derived from note titles, verify filename safety beyond whitespace replacement: after converting title whitespace to underscores and appending `.md`, check whether the title contains path-sensitive or disallowed filename characters (especially `/` or `\\`, and any other characters rejected by the documented file-system API/environment). Sanitize those characters before `file_system.create_file(...)` so one note title cannot accidentally create nested paths or cause file-creation failures.
[vc-00096] [vc-new] Before creating a Spotify playlist after checking for an existing exact-title match, verify the title-check scan paginated `show_playlist_library(access_token, page_index)` to an empty page (or found a match and stopped intentionally). Do not assume the batch succeeded just because later directory listings look plausible.

[vc-00083] [vc-new] Before the first `spotify.like_song(...)` call in a 'like all songs from followed artists' task, compute and log the set relationship between `discovered_song_ids` and `liked_song_ids`: at minimum print `len(discovered_song_ids)`, `len(liked_song_ids)`, `len(discovered_song_ids & liked_song_ids)`, and `len(discovered_song_ids - liked_song_ids)`. Then apply the filter against `show_song(song_id)['release_date']` year values.

Do not proceed if any of these values come from a manual assignment or a variable whose source is not visible from prior API output in the current run.

[vc-00014] For paginated library endpoints, verify termination by looping page_index until an empty list is returned; do not assume a fixed number of pages or infer completion from page size/default page_limit.

[vc-00011] For cross-library Spotify count tasks, before submission sanity-check coverage by recording how many song IDs were discovered from each source separately (song library, album library, playlist library) and confirm the final answer is the size of the unioned set, not the sum of per-source counts.
[vc-00010] Before submitting a top-N filtered song answer, verify how many songs actually match the filter. If fewer than N matches exist, return all matching titles (not padded or invented); otherwise return exactly N.
[vc-00009] Before submitting a top-N answer, verify the final ranked list contains the requested number of items (or all available matches if fewer exist) and that the comma-separated output is built from that exact final slice.
[vc-00005] Before submitting: confirm you aggregated songs from all three library sources (songs, albums, playlists), deduplicated repeated song IDs, filtered using normalized genre text, sorted by descending `play_count`, and returned exactly 4 song titles as a comma-separated string with no extra text.
[vc-00006] If multiple songs can tie on the ranking metric, apply a deterministic secondary sort (e.g. title ascending) so the answer is stable and reproducible.


## TROUBLESHOOTING AND PITFALLS
[tp-00115] In paginated Venmo reminder workflows, prefer compact debugging output after schema is confirmed: print page counts, candidate counts, target `payment_request_id`s, and any non-success reminder responses instead of full contact/request pages. This reduces context-noise risk without sacrificing verification.
[tp-00082] [tp-new] In large paginated Spotify workflows, avoid printing every full page or every mutation result unless needed for debugging. Prefer compact summaries (e.g. page counts, sampled IDs, failure-only outputs) so tool context is less likely to truncate before the final verification/completion steps.
